commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=7453f8a6db8f5edc56b072437ce988742c5cac83)
commands.review_summary:
The branch tightens task owner access so blank normalized task or caller owner keys cannot authorize owner-scoped task reads, and adds regression tests for blank callers and restored blank-owner task records.

PR surface: Source +4, Tests +75. Total +79 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main normalizes blank task and caller owner keys to undefined and then compares them for equality. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The next step is contributor proof plus maintainer acceptance of the fail-closed legacy-row behavior; there is no narrow automated code repair required from this review.

target.title: Require non-empty owner keys for task access
target.body:
## Summary
- require both task owner keys and caller owner keys to normalize to non-empty values before owner-scoped task access succeeds
- add regression coverage for blank caller keys and restored legacy session tasks with blank owner keys

## Tests
- ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/tasks/task-owner-access.test.ts
- ./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/tasks/task-owner-access.ts src/tasks/task-owner-access.test.ts

Note: initial `pnpm vitest ...` and pre-commit dependency checks attempted `pnpm install`, but sharp's install script failed because node-gyp is not available in this worktree. I completed dependency linking with `pnpm install --ignore-scripts` and ran the scoped binaries directly.
target.author: marksurfas

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
